operating_state: Total revenue remained flat at $6.8 billion as higher commodity prices offset lower sales volumes. Oil and gas segment earnings increased to $1.7 billion from $1.2 billion driven by higher domestic prices. Average daily sales volumes decreased to 1,391 Mboe from 1,463 Mboe due to ethane rejection and weather-related Permian delays, though year-over-year volumes increased 18% from the CrownRock Acquisition.
cost_structure: Depreciation, depletion and amortization increased to $1.9 billion from $1.7 billion primarily from increased Permian Basin sales volumes following the CrownRock Acquisition. Chemical segment earnings declined due to lower caustic soda and polyvinyl chloride prices combined with higher ethylene and natural gas costs. Operating cash flow from continuing operations increased to $2.1 billion from $2.0 billion despite higher working capital usage from timing of federal tax payments.
material_events: Occidental redeemed senior notes and repaid term loan debt totaling $515 million during Q1 2025. The company completed $1.3 billion in divestitures of non-core oil and gas assets. In March 2025, Occidental offered to exercise outstanding warrants at $21.30 per share, subsequently issuing 41.9 million shares in April 2025 for proceeds of approximately $890 million used to repay near-term debt maturities.
macro_exposures: President Trump announced sweeping tariff policy in April 2025 imposing 10% baseline tariff on most imports with higher reciprocal rates for certain nations, though significant rates are paused for 90 days during trade negotiations. Occidental cannot assess final impact but notes tariffs may increase supplier costs and affect OxyChem's import-export operations. Oil price volatility continues from geopolitical risks and OPEC actions.
forward_outlook: Occidental maintains strategic priorities to preserve asset base integrity, deliver sustainable growing dividends, and prioritize excess cash flow for deleveraging until principal debt falls below $15 billion. Capital expenditures were $1.9 billion in Q1 2025 compared to $1.8 billion in Q1 2024. The company expects cash on hand, operating cash flows, and available credit facilities to meet obligations over the next 12 months.
uncertainty_profile: Principal debt outstanding totaled $22.1 billion after recent repayments, with no maturities in 2025 and $2.9 billion in 2026. Zero Coupon bonds can be put to Occidental in October 2025 for approximately $381 million if exercised in whole. Environmental compliance costs are expected to rise with ongoing remedial activities under federal laws that may apply retroactively regardless of fault.
